info.title: intraktible Decision API
info.description: The public integration contract for intraktible: execute decision flows, read decision history, manage flows as code, and drive the deployment lifecycle (deploy / promote) for CI/CD. Other administrative endpoints (RBAC, audit, secrets) are out of scope of this document. Authenticate every request with an API key in the X-Api-Key header.

tags.name: flows
tags.description: List, create, read, and import flows.

tags.name: deployments
tags.description: Deploy and promote versions across environments (CI/CD).

post.summary: Execute a flow
post.description: Runs the live version of the flow (champion, or A/B challenger) in the given environment against the supplied data and records the decision.

200.description: The decision was recorded (a flow whose logic errors is a recorded 'failed' decision, not an HTTP error).

post.summary: Batch-execute a flow over a dataset
post.description: Runs each row of a dataset through the recorded decide path (capped at 500 rows).

post.summary: Stream-execute a flow over a large NDJSON dataset
post.description: The large-job batch path: the request body is NDJSON (one input object per line) and the response is NDJSON streamed one result per line, flushed as each row decides. No dataset is held in memory and there is no row cap, so it scales to very large jobs. entity_type / entity_key are query parameters applied to every row. Each row is a real recorded decision (history, metrics, audit), like /decide/batch.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Entity type recorded for every row.

parameters.name: entity_key
parameters.description: Input field each row's entity id is read from.

200.description: NDJSON: one decision result per line, streamed.

get.summary: Generated OpenAPI contract for one flow
get.description: Returns a self-contained OpenAPI 3.1 document describing this flow's decide and decide/batch endpoints, with the flow's latest published input schema as the request data schema. Point codegen / Swagger UI at it for a flow-specific contract.

post.summary: Define (or redefine) a predictive model
post.description: Registers a named model evaluated deterministically by the engine and referenced from a Predict node. spec.kind is logistic | gbm | expression | external: logistic {intercept,coefficients:{feature:weight}} · gbm {base?,link?:logit,trees:[{feature,threshold,left,right}|{leaf:true,value}]} · expression {expr:<expr-lang over features>} · external {endpoint:<https url>,timeout_ms?} (BYO served model — the shell POSTs the features and reads a {score,probability} response, egress-guarded).

post.summary: Train a logistic model from a labelled dataset
post.description: Fits a logistic-regression model (deterministic batch gradient descent, k-fold cross-validation, feature importance) from a labelled dataset and defines it under name — a servable model, no hand-authored coefficients. Returns the training report.

201.description: The model was trained and registered; body carries the training report.

get.description: Returns the model's distribution of predicted probabilities (deciles) and, once a baseline is captured, the PSI vs that baseline (<0.1 stable, 0.1–0.25 moderate, >0.25 significant), plus per-input-feature COVARIATE drift (features[]: standardized mean shift + variance ratio vs the baseline). ?window=Nd measures only the most recent N day-buckets (a windowed view; absent = all-time). firing is set when PSI exceeds the configured monitor threshold.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Recent-window size, e.g. "7d" or "30d"; absent = all-time.

200.description: Drift report: count, decile histogram, PSI when a baseline exists, and per-feature covariate drift.

get.summary: Live performance reconciled against recorded actuals
get.description: Returns the model's live performance measured from recorded ground-truth outcomes: calibration (realized positive rate per predicted decile), accuracy, Brier score, and realized AUC.

200.description: Performance report (zero-valued until actuals are recorded).

post.summary: Record a realized outcome for a model prediction
post.description: Reconciles a realized ground-truth label (0/1) with the probability the model predicted, feeding the performance report.

decision_id.description: Optional lineage back to the decision.

post.summary: Capture the current prediction distribution as the drift baseline
Set (or clear) the model's PSI drift alert threshold
post.description: When set (threshold > 0), the drift report's `firing` flag is true once PSI exceeds it; threshold <= 0 clears the monitor.

post.summary: Record validation evidence for the model's current version
post.description: Attaches a validation record (dataset, named metrics, validator, notes, pass/fail) to the model's current version. Evidence an approver reviews; not itself the gate. Editor role.

post.summary: Request four-eyes approval of the model's current version
post.description: The maker side of model four-eyes: proposes the current version for review. Fails if the model is unknown, already approved at this version, or already has a pending request. Editor role.

post.summary: Approve a pending model-approval request (checker)
post.description: The checker side: a different actor — neither the requester nor the version's author — approves, marking the version approved for serving. Approver role.

post.summary: Reject a pending model-approval request (checker)
Explain a flow graph in plain language
post.description: Authoring copilot (backed by the configured AI provider). Returns a plain-language explanation of the posted {graph}. 503 if no provider is configured.

post.summary: Suggest decision logic from a natural-language requirement
Generate an applyable flow graph from a requirement
post.description: Asks the AI for a structured flow graph and validates it server-side (ValidateGraph) before returning, so the builder only ever applies a graph the engine accepts. 422 when the model can't produce a valid flow.

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Only decisions started at or after this RFC3339 time (alias: since).

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Only decisions started at or before this RFC3339 time (alias: until).

parameters.name: include_node_results
parameters.description: Set false to omit each decision's per-node trace for a lighter response (the trace is always available on the decision-detail endpoint).

200.description: A page of decisions, newest first.
The decision record with its node-level trace.
All flows for the caller's tenant.

post.summary: Create a flow

200.description: The flow with its versions and deployments.

patch.description: Sets a flow's mutable details (name and/or description). PATCH semantics: an absent field is left unchanged. Requires the editor role.

200.description: The flow's resolved details after the update.

post.summary: Import a flow as code
post.description: Upserts a flow from an exported document: creates it when the slug is new, otherwise publishes the graph as a new version. Re-importing identical content is a no-op (200, published=false).

200.description: No change (the live latest version already matches).

201.description: A flow and/or version was written.

post.summary: Import many flows at once
post.description: Imports a bundle of flow-as-code documents (a GitOps repo). Best-effort: a failing flow is reported in its result rather than aborting the rest.

200.description: A per-flow report with rollup counts.

post.summary: Deploy a version to an environment
post.description: Makes a published version live in an environment (a direct deploy). Requires the approver role.

201.description: The version is live in the environment.

post.summary: Promote a live version up the environment chain
post.description: Ships the live version of `from` up to `to`. A non-production target deploys directly; production opens a maker-checker request. A promotion gate (firing monitors / failing assertions) can block it unless `force` is allowed for the stage. Requires the approver role.

200.description: Promoted directly, or a maker-checker request was opened (pending).
The resolved identity, scope, and role.

post.summary: Publish a new flow version (the primary authoring write). Node positions are optional — omit them and the server fills a deterministic swimlane auto-layout.

get.summary: Live decision metrics for a flow.
Get a flow's stored assertion cases.

post.summary: Run a flow's assertions against a version.
Replay a dataset through a published version (no recorded decision).
Sweep one input field across a range and report disposition transitions.
Define a monitor rule.
Evaluate monitors now and push the firing set to webhooks.
Snapshot the disposition mix as the drift baseline.

get.summary: Per-bucket distribution drift vs the baseline.
Get the per-environment shadow version + comparison report.

put.summary: Set the shadow version for an environment.

post.summary: Open a maker-checker deployment request (production).
Approve a deployment request (a different user).
Reject a deployment request.
Promote a population into pre-approvals via the decide path.
Create a policy bound to a flow.
Publish a policy version (disposition bands).
Backtest a policy over a dataset (records nothing).
Grant a pre-approval.

get.summary: List the case queue (filter by status/type/assignee).

post.summary: Open a review case.

get.summary: Get one case with its notes + audit log.

post.summary: Define an agent.
Run an agent synchronously.
Register a notification webhook.

get.description: Counts by status, unassigned, and SLA buckets over the same filtered set as the case list (filter by status/type/assignee).

post.summary: Emit SLA-breach events for overdue open cases
post.description: The push side of SLA tracking — a scheduler/cron calls it. Returns the breached case ids.

post.summary: Assign (or reassign) a case
post.description: Claims a case for an assignee. Assigning an already owned case without reassign=true is refused, so one claim cannot silently overwrite another.

reassign.description: Take the case away from its current assignee.

post.summary: Set a case's status
Add a note to a case
Record a custom entity

get.summary: Compute an entity's features (live, or point-in-time via as_of)

parameters.name: as_of
parameters.description: RFC3339 instant to reproduce the point-in-time feature values as of the past (only events that had occurred by then). Omit for a live read served through the materialized cache.

post.summary: Record a custom event
Define a feature
post.description: Declares an aggregation (e.g. count/sum/avg over a window) over an entity type's events, injected under 'features' at decide time.

post.summary: Define a connector
post.description: Registers a named external data connector. Credential fields in config are sealed under the keyring (or redacted) before the definition is recorded.

config.description: Type-specific configuration; credential fields are never returned in plaintext.

get.description: Credential config fields are masked at the read boundary.
The catalog of built-in connector types and their config templates.

post.summary: Invoke a connector
post.description: Invokes a defined connector (the external effect) and records the result as an event, so the response is auditable and replay-stable.

200.description: The connector response, plus the recorded fetch id.

post.summary: Resume a suspended decision
post.description: Un-pauses a decision suspended at a durable human task, injecting the reviewer's outcome so the flow runs on to a terminal. The outcome fields are merged into the decision's record. Carries the same environment scope gate as the decide path.

post.summary: Counterfactual levers for an unfavorable decision
post.description: Answers "what would flip this?": for an unfavorable decision, searches each numeric input field for the minimal single-field change that re-runs to a more favorable disposition. Records nothing; the search is bounded.

200.description: The disposition and the ordered flips found.

get.summary: Export a flow version as code / diagram
get.description: Renders a flow version: ?format=mermaid (the default) | mermaid-state | bpmn | dot | json (round-trippable, the flow-as-code document /v1/flows/import accepts). ?version=N defaults to the latest. Returned as a file attachment.

get.summary: Get a flow's SLOs and current attainment

200.description: The configured objectives (null when none) and current attainment.

put.description: Records the flow's objectives (a zeroed body clears them).

window_days.description: Rolling window in days to measure attainment over (0 = all-time; max 90).

get.description: Across the flow's recent completed decisions, how many hit each node of its current published graph, plus the disposition tally.

200.description: Per-node hit counts and the disposition distribution.

post.summary: Reachability / dead-path coverage report
post.description: Generates a deterministic fan of synthetic inputs over the numeric fields the graph references, runs each through the pure engine, and tallies per-node hits, per-branch hits, and the disposition distribution. Nodes/branches with zero hits are reported as dead. Records nothing.

post.summary: Schedule a deploy for a future time
post.description: Queues a deployment for a future time (optionally time-boxed with until, which auto-reverts). Requires the environment scope and any per-flow grant.

until.description: RFC3339; when set, the deploy is time-boxed and auto-reverts.

post.summary: Roll an environment back to its previous version
post.description: Reverts an environment to its previous live version (instant rollback). Requires the environment scope and the per-flow grant.

post.summary: Grant a caller change-control on a flow
post.description: Grants an actor the per-flow change-control permission, optionally scoped to one environment (omit for any, "*").

environment.description: Environment the grant applies to; omit for any ("*").

get.summary: Read a pre-approval for an entity

post.summary: Revoke a pre-approval
Record a subject's lawful basis for a purpose
post.description: A purpose-limitation ledger entry (GDPR Art. 6/7, GLBA). subject and purpose are required; basis defaults to 'consent' but for credit decisioning is usually contract/legitimate_interest; expires_at is optional and must be in the future. evidence optionally backs the record with how the authorization was obtained, a tamper-evident content hash of the signed artifact (the bytes stay in the controller's own store), and the disclosure version shown.

reference.description: Locator for the signed artifact in the controller's system of record.

content_hash.description: Hex digest of the artifact (tamper-evidence); requires hash_algo.

notice_version.description: The disclosure/notice version shown to the subject.

post.summary: Withdraw a subject's consent for a purpose

get.summary: List a subject's consents across purposes
get.description: The subject is an opaque string (the decide integration keys it as 'type/id'), so it is a query parameter.

get.summary: Whether a subject has active consent for a purpose

200.description: The active flag plus the full record when one exists.

get.description: Any subject can carry a thread — deployment requests, decisions, cases.

post.summary: Append a comment to a subject

parent_id.description: Reply to another comment in the thread.

get.description: A review-capable caller (operator+) also sees unassigned tasks in the shared queue.

post.summary: Mark a notification read

get.description: The tenant's durable API tokens (admin-gated). Secrets are never returned on list.

post.summary: Mint a managed API key
post.description: Creates a durable token. A caller cannot mint a key broader than their own credential — neither a wider environment scope nor a higher role (403). The secret is returned once, on creation.

actor.description: The identity the key acts as.

scope.description: Environment the key may call (default sandbox).

201.description: The key and its one-time secret.

post.summary: Rotate a managed API key
post.description: Mints a fresh secret for the key, optionally keeping the old one working for grace_seconds. Subject to the same scope+role ceiling as create (403).

grace_seconds.description: Seconds the previous secret keeps working (>= 0).

200.description: The rotated key and its new secret.

get.description: The tenant-scoped audit log, filterable and newest-first. ?format=csv exports the filtered set as a file.

200.description: A page of audit entries, or a CSV export.

get.description: The live MRM report inventorying the tenant's decision logic. ?format=csv (inventory spreadsheet) or ?format=md (filed document); default JSON.

200.description: The MRM report as JSON, CSV, or Markdown.

get.description: Adverse-impact ratio (four-fifths rule, ECOA / Reg B) of favorable-outcome rates across a protected-class attribute, folded from the flow's recorded decisions. flow and attribute are required. ?format=csv or ?format=md; default JSON.

parameters.name: attribute
parameters.description: Dot-path into the decision input naming the protected class, e.g. applicant.gender.

200.description: The disparate-impact report as JSON, CSV, or Markdown.
The flow's fair-lending config (defaults when unset).

put.description: Declares the protected-class attribute (dot-path into the decision input), the favorable disposition, and the AIR threshold (0 = four-fifths default). Admin only.

200.description: The workspace creditor identification (defaults when unset).

put.description: The creditor identification an ECOA / Reg B adverse-action notice requires. creditor_name is required. Admin only.

cra_name.description: Consumer reporting agency, required by FCRA §615(a) only for a report-based notice.

get.summary: Generate an adverse-action notice for a declined decision
get.description: Renders the ECOA / Reg B notice from the decision's recorded reason codes and the workspace creditor settings. Markdown by default; ?format=json returns { decision_id, notice, issuance? }. ?consumer_report=true adds the FCRA §615(a) disclosures (the CRA must be configured). 400 when the decision is not a decline, carries no reason codes, or the workspace has no creditor configured.

parameters.name: consumer_report
parameters.description: Include the FCRA §615(a) disclosures (decision was based on a consumer report).

200.description: The adverse-action notice as Markdown, or JSON when ?format=json.

post.summary: Record that a declined decision's adverse-action notice was served
post.description: Renders the notice (exactly as the download does), stores its content hash, and records the issuance — the durable ECOA/Reg B proof that the applicant was served (who, when, by what method, citing which reasons). based_on_consumer_report adds the FCRA §615(a) disclosures and requires the CRA to be configured. Operator only.

get.summary: List declined decisions and their notice status (the work queue)
get.description: The tenant's declined decisions and whether each has had its adverse-action notice issued, with the age of the decision (the 30-day-clock surface). ?status=pending returns only unissued declines; ?status=issued only served ones.

200.description: The declined decisions and their notice status.

get.summary: Get an agent's stored eval cases

post.summary: Run an agent's stored eval cases
post.description: Runs the agent's stored eval cases against a version (records nothing).

get.summary: Run an agent, streaming output as Server-Sent Events
get.description: Runs the agent and streams provider chunks as SSE (text/event-stream): `chunk` events during the run, then a terminal `error` event on failure. The prompt is a query parameter.

200.description: The SSE stream of run chunks.

get.summary: Run an agent over a WebSocket
get.description: Upgrades to a WebSocket (HTTP 101) and streams provider chunks as messages for the duration of the run. The prompt is a query parameter. WebSocket framing is outside the JSON contract.

post.summary: Escalate an agent run to a review case
post.description: Opens a review case from an agent run (defaults to case type agent_review when unspecified).

get.description: Token usage across runs, plus derived cost when a per-model price table is configured.

data.description: The decision input, validated against the version's input schema.

entity_type.description: Optional Context Layer entity type; its features are injected under 'features'.

preview.description: Run the flow without recording anything (no decision event, case, metrics, or audit) — the builder's "Test decision". The response carries the same shape but an empty decision_id.

mock_data.description: Overrides for pre-resolved engine namespaces (features/connect/ai/predict) used when previewing, so a flow can be tested without live providers.

metadata.description: Opaque caller metadata recorded with the decision.

control.description: Per-call execution controls passed through to the engine.

status.description: "suspended" when the flow paused at a durable human task (resume it via POST /v1/decisions/{decision_id}/resume).

disposition.description: The policy's automated outcome, when a policy is bound.

disposition_reason.description: What assigned the disposition (a policy band, or "pre-approval honored").

preapproval_id.description: The grant this decision was served from, when it was honored from a pre-approval (evidence the flow never ran).

description.description: Prose describing what the flow decides.
Optional prose describing what the flow decides (max 2000 chars).

UpdateFlowRequest.description: A partial update: an absent field is left unchanged. The slug and version history are immutable.

name.description: New display name (must be non-empty when present).

description.description: New description (max 2000 chars; an empty string clears it).

name.description: The resolved display name after the update.

description.description: The resolved description after the update.

challenger_pct.description: Percent of traffic routed to the challenger.

from.description: Source environment whose live version is promoted.

force.description: Override the promotion gate where the stage allows it.

promoted.description: True when the version deployed directly.

pending.description: True when a maker-checker request was opened (production).

scope.description: Environment the key may call: "sandbox", "production", or "*" (any). A scoped key is rejected (403) on a non-matching environment's decide endpoints.

NodePosition.description: Saved canvas coordinate (presentation only; the runtime ignores it). Omit it and the server fills a deterministic auto-layout.

config.description: Opaque, node-type-specific configuration; the API stores it verbatim and each node engine validates it at decide time. Shapes by type: input {} · assignment {assignments:[{target,expr}]} · rule {rules:[{when,then:[{target,expr}]}]} · split {condition:<bool expr>} (routes to the "yes"/"no" branch edges) · scorecard {factors:[{when,weight}]} · decision_table {hit:first|unique|any|rule_order|collect, aggregate?:sum|min|max|count (collect only), rows:[{when,outputs:[{target,expr}]}]} · 2d_matrix {rows,cols,cells} · reason {reasons:[{when,code,description}]} · manual_review {company_name,case_type,sla_days} (expressions) · code {source:<Starlark>} · connect {connector:<name>} · ai {agent:<name>,prompt,output} · predict {model:<name>,output} (injects predict.<output> {score,probability}) · output {fields:[...]}. expr/when/condition are expr-lang expressions over the decision input; the code node runs Starlark (see docs/EXPRESSIONS.md).

branch.description: Label for a conditional edge (e.g. "yes"/"no" out of a split); empty for unconditional flow.

Graph.description: A flow version's node/edge structure. Must have exactly one input node and at least one output node, unique node ids, and be acyclic; edges must reference existing distinct nodes (no self-loops).

etag.description: SHA-256 of {graph, input_schema}; an identical re-publish is a detectable no-op.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for the decide input (optional).
JSON Schema for the decide input (optional).

AssertionsDoc.description: Stored input→expected test cases for a flow.

MonitorRule.description: A threshold rule over a flow's live metrics.

PromotionStagePolicy.description: Gates a promotion into one environment.

PolicySpec.description: A policy version: disposition bands + default.

PreApprovalGrant.description: A durable, time-boxed pre-decision for one entity.

fields.description: Case-insensitive field names masked at the read boundary.
